title: The Counter-Offer Conversation
description: Push back politely on a low offer. Use specific data and a clear ask, not emotion.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks for considering us. The team is excited. So — what do you think of the offer?

templates.text: I really appreciate it. The role and the team are a strong fit for me.
templates.hint_zh: 非常感謝。職務跟團隊都很適合我。

templates.text: Yes, the base is below the market range I've seen for this level.
templates.hint_zh: 是的,底薪比我看到這個層級的市場行情還低。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We benchmarked across the bay area. What numbers are you looking at?

templates.text: Levels.fyi shows the median around 180k base for a senior role at a comparable company.
templates.hint_zh: Levels.fyi 顯示同等公司、同等級的資深職位中位數約 18 萬底薪。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That's the top of the range we modeled. What's your ask?

templates.text: 175 base, plus an extra 20k in equity over four years.
templates.hint_zh: 底薪 17.5 萬,加四年內多 2 萬美金股權。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Let me take that to the hiring committee. Anything else if I can move on the equity?

templates.text: A signing bonus would help offset the relocation. I can be flexible on the split.
templates.hint_zh: 簽約獎金可以幫我抵搬遷成本。組合可以彈性。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Understood. I'll get back to you by Thursday.

templates.text: Thank you. I look forward to the conversation.
